perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT hereby waives and appoints OWNER to assert on RECIPIENT’S behalf RECIPIENT’S moral rights or any equivalent rights regarding the form or extent of any alteration to the Work (including, without limitation, removal or destruction) or the making of any derivative works based on the Work, including, without limitation, photographs, drawings or other visual reproductions or the Work, in any medium, for OWNER'S purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT may choose to waive and appoint OWNER to assert on RECIPIENT’S behalf RECIPIENT’S moral rights or any equivalent rights regarding the form or extent of any alteration to the Work (including, without limitation, removal or destruction) or the making of any derivative works based on the Work, including, without limitation, photographs, drawings or other visual reproductions or the Work, in any medium, for OWNER'S purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The term "may choose" makes RECIPIENT no longer binded to waiver, which creates uncertainty regarding moral rights to the work. In one instance RECIPIENT hereby waive and appoints and in the other, the RECIPIENT may or may not waive. This creates an uncertainty about whether moral rights are fully transferred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. If the Work is one to which the provisions of 17 U.S.C. § 106A apply, RECIPIENT may choose to waive and appoint OWNER to assert on RECIPIENT’S behalf RECIPIENT’S moral rights or any equivalent rights regarding the form or extent of any alteration to the Work (including, without limitation, removal or destruction) or the making of any derivative works based on the Work, including, without limitation, photographs, drawings or other visual reproductions or the Work, in any medium, for OWNER'S purposes. 4. RECIPIENT agrees to execute all papers and to perform such other proper acts as OWNER may deem necessary to secure for OWNER or its designee the rights herein assigned.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "RECIPIENT hereby waives and appoints", meaning the RECIPIENT is obligated to waive their rights. The changed text, however, introduces "RECIPIENT may choose to waive and appoint", making the waiver optional. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text which states "RECIPIENT may choose to waive and appoint" in section 3. In section 4, it is expected that RECIPIENT agrees to execute all papers to secure rights herein assigned, but since the RECIPIENT may or may not waive, there is uncertainty, therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT agrees that should RECIPIENT breach a ny of the promises contained in this Agreement that OWNER would suffer irreparable harm and OWNER w ould be without adequate remedy at law and that OWNER may obtain injunctive relief, including specific performance of the Agreement, as well as monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT agrees that should RECIPIENT breach a ny of the promises contained in this Agreement that OWNER would suffer irreparable harm and OWNER w ould be without adequate remedy at law and that OWNER may obtain injunctive relief at OWNER's discretion. No monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement will be given.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentions the RECIPIENT may obtain specific performance of the agreement, as well as monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’s breach of this Agreement. However the changed text mentions, No monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement will be given. This creates a contradiction with the section, on how the OWNER can get monetary funds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. DAMAGES AND SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE RECIPIENT agrees that should RECIPIENT breach a ny of the promises contained in this Agreement that OWNER would suffer irreparable harm and OWNER w ould be without adequate remedy at law and that OWNER may obtain injunctive relief at OWNER's discretion. No monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement will be given.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the OWNER to obtain monetary awards for damages suffered, however the changed text explicitly states that 'No monetary award for damages suffered by OWNER for RECIPIENT’S breach of this Agreement will be given.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, as it removes the possibility of monetary awards that were previously mentioned, creating a clear conflict and Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding this paragraph, RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations conc erning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding this paragraph, RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations concerning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION, only if the obligations are written.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a limitation to prior obligations. RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations concerning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION, only if the obligations are written. However, the prior version of this section does not mention that RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations only if they are written. It is expected from the RECIPIENT to honor all prior obligations conc erning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VIII. MERGER/MODIFICATION IN WRITING RECIPIENT agrees that this Agreement shall supersede all prior agreements and shall not be modified by either party except in writing and by agreement betw een both parties. Notwithstanding this paragraph, RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations concerning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION, only if the obligations are written.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition (only if the obligations are written) that restricts the honoring of prior confidentiality obligations. The original text states that RECIPIENT shall honor all prior obligations concerning confidentiality of OWNER’S confidential INFORMATION, creating a contradiction with the changed text. The contradicted text also includes the changed text so it clearly contradicts itself. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT hereby acknowledges that OWNER shall retain all right, title, and interest in all trademarks, trade dress, and good will that results from the INFORMATION or any use or offer to sell thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT hereby acknowledges that OWNER shall retain all right, title, and interest in all trademarks, trade dress, and good will that results from the INFORMATION or any use or offer to sell thereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the owner's ownership of trademarks. The original clause states OWNER shall retain all right, title, and interest in all trademarks, trade dress, and good will. The updated clause allows for exceptions, creating a potential conflict. The phase unless otherwise agreed in writing is vague and makes it unclear when the right, title, and interest in all trademarks, trade dress, and good will transfers. This introduces a significant ambiguity regarding ownership of intellectual property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Ownership of Trademarks RECIPIENT hereby acknowledges that OWNER shall retain all right, title, and interest in all trademarks, trade dress, and good will that results from the INFORMATION or any use or offer to sell thereof, unless otherwise agreed in writing. IV. COVENANT NOT TO SUE RECIPIENT shall not institute any action or suit at law or in equity against OWNER, nor institute, prosecute or in any way aid in the institution or prosecution of any claim, demand, action, or cause of action arising out of the INFORMATION or any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY thereof, including but not limited to, claim, demand, action, or cause of action for in validating any INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of OWNER.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed in writing' to the original clause regarding ownership of trademarks, which originally stated the OWNER 'shall retain all right, title, and interest'. This introduces a significant ambiguity because the contradicted text, which repeats the changed text, now has the same ambiguity. The added exception directly contradicts the original intent of the clause by creating a loophole that allows for ownership to be transferred, creating a structural flaw that creates an in-text contradiction.
